title: swipe
description: Swipe (or scroll) a surface in a direction or between two points. The direction is the virtual finger's motion: swiping up moves content up, revealing content further down the page. Works on app and browser surfaces.

direction.title: Swipe (simple)
direction.description: Direction the virtual finger moves. `up` reveals content below; `left` reveals content to the right (for example, the next carousel card).

point.title: Point
point.description: A pixel coordinate on the surface, measured from its top-left corner (0, 0).

x.description: Horizontal position in pixels from the left edge of the surface (the app window, or the browser viewport).

y.description: Vertical position in pixels from the top edge of the surface (the app window, or the browser viewport).

duration.description: Duration of the swipe movement in milliseconds.

surface.description: The browser window/tab or app window this step acts on. Omit to act on the active surface — the most recently opened, focused, or explicitly targeted surface, whatever its kind (a background process can't be swiped). Specifying a surface switches the active surface: it stays targeted for later surface-less steps. App surfaces use the object form ({ "app": … }).

distance.description: How far to swipe, as a fraction of the surface's height (for up/down) or width (for left/right).
